about character strength in this game
even if tawil is weaker than ghelot
she can still clear endgame
given enough investment
what matters is that you play with characters you enjoy
and that you put together teams you like playing
so its similair to limbus company in that regard?
the game has a lot of avenues of investment that u can put on your chars to make them stronger if they're struggling
the systems are rich and complex
so u can make people who are normally weak
shine
if you put them in specific situations
very few characters are truly unviable or worthless
additionally
the endgame needs you to have 5 teams minimum
to full clear it
so even if other teams are stronger
that doesnt matter
your tawil will find a place
among those 5 teams

also, the endgame doesn't have a turn limit
so people who play slower, or need more time to ramp up their damage
can still be viable and clear

Hi, i'm a couple weeks in and looking for pull advice. Which banner should i pull on, or should i start saving up now for future banners? I'm sitting on about 100 pulls for limited banners atm. Thanks <:Qing_Heart:1512807790069022811>

For an aqueor team with current units, Corp+Aurita is solid for discard, Murphy helps protect, further buffs tentacle damage, from there maybe Celeste for the healing

Would end up kind of a stall discard comp, as you scale tentacle damage into a big Aurita exalt

Are you struggling at all currently, or just looking to kind of future proof when progressing more?

Generally a good idea to try to get 1 of each character, can use the monthly dupe to get any character upgraded eventually, plus they are generally pretty generous with additional pulls
for future progression really
i'll aim for a copy of each limited in that case

Tawil, g.Murphy, Pontos, Castor, g.Doll all super solid and can carry teams

Looking at what you have right now you could actually run thais/saya/salvador/fill

Just replicate salvador's coffin drops over and over, can be a bit harder to use than a standard salvador comp cause of p.caro mechanics with saya

For future, Castor+Jenkins works well

I'd suggest pulling blue for realm study, mainly to try to get Goliath, you have Miryam if you get a Vortice from there or g.murphy you'd have a solid tentacle comp
Hiii, im sorta new to the  game, should i keep pulling for corpo oe or is it better for me to invest in other units/teamcomps?

Focus one comp you enjoy, big difficulty spike at ch.7 and you don't really need multiple teams until later on

If you get the units you can upgrade them later with monthly dupe from shop -> Exchange -> Sediment - Prototype Horizon

They asked who to look toward in the future, not current banner 😄

Goliath realm study, not vortice or g.murphy

Saying if they get Goliath, they have everything they need, sans Vortice or g.murphy

Since your Pollux is already at +1, it wouldn't be a bad idea to get him to OE 
Pollux's wheel is also very much worth getting, though I'm assuming you already have that 
Outside of that, unless you're interested in playing either GHelot or discard teams, I'd suggest just saving for now
There are pretty much 0 reasons to get Goliath without an invested GMurphy in your roster
You missed the future part of this conversation I feel like <:whenlifegetsatyou:749033396025557063> heh
I've read through it, but the advice to aim for Goliath doesn't really make sense

because they can make a solid team out of it for later with Goliath and a g.murphy, either friend, or if they grab her in a *future* banner

That way when they get there, they have Goliath, and aren't wondering who else to put on their team, or why their tentacle team doesn't slap as hard as expected

They already have a strong team in the face of Pollux - Saya - Thais - Aigis 
There are no guarantees of GMurphy rerun anytime soon, not even mentioning the fact that making her a viable dps requires a lot more than a single copy

I'm not saying invest in Goliath, I'm saying for your Pure Cores, of which, none are used on their main team still - Getting a Goliath is not a bad idea for the future <:facepalm:1203448560369012766> 

and for event / p.dive etc, they can EASILY borrow a fully whaled out g.murphy, drop a goliath ult and do a million damage.

Yes, and I'm saying Goliath is not worth picking up without an invested GMurphy already in your roster, there are plenty of better ways of using your standard pulls 

Or, you know, just borrow Mouchette and do that without any need for teammates

Sure, all of that works fine right now, Mouch saya is very strong. can also borrow a whaled mouch for event / p.dive

Still not a use of pure cores, yee getting titans maxed right now will be a better benefit than Goliath - In the future, considering they already have a team they like, and can use that without having to use a supp, like for d-zone, can easily go, fuck it, goliath, miryam, g.murphy supp, go brr
This is why I asked first, are they struggling, or wanting to look toward future teams and shit

GMurphy needs at least E3, ideally OE to be a good DTide dps 
They are very likely to get their Goliath while pulling for her, if they choose to do so

<:cb_sideeye:1237762308592373862>  I had OE g.murphy before goliath, because I didn't pull on realm study 😄

To be fair, she is the main DPS I've invested in, and also as someone who doesn't put money into this, more likely to pull g.murphy on banner, and then you have.... 10+ other options that are not Goliath
Not so likely to get a Goliath.
What
What 10+ options are we talking about
It's like 5, I'm exaggerating <:facepalm:1203448560369012766>
Not the damn point lol

I have no idea what point you're trying to make 
As I said, there are no reasons to aim for Goliath without GMurphy, and even if you do plan to get GMurphy, there's a good chance to roll Goliath in the process, so that isn't a good reason to aim for him either

You're making the assumption that they will pull a lot on g.murphy, not get one copy and use dupes in the future

Correct, if they do not plan to get g.murphy, or a vortice or someone that cares a lot about tentacle damage, Goliath is completely pointless, this is why I made the suggestion I did. They have enough other characters to make that kind of team work, the only realm study they are missing is Goliath.

So for the future, looking to pick up a Goliath isn't a bad idea to prepare, which was the question.

ADDITIONALLY - This is over looking that again, they can borrow a whaled g.murphy, right this exact second, and in that case, Goliath can help with that.

Yes, they can borrow a whaled Mouch too, who slots on their current team well, or quite a few others for other teams, I'm pointing out options.

Right this exact second, best use of pure cores, probably goliath's wheel still, cool.

embeds.description:
Shub-Niggurath is a fictional deity created by H. P. Lovecraft. She is often associated with the phrase "The Black Goat of the Woods with a Thousand Young". The only other name by which Lovecraft referred to her was "Lord of the Wood" in his story The Whisperer in Darkness.
